learning_objectives.title: Variables, Data Types, and Assignment
learning_objectives.description: Students can declare variables, assign values, and use Python's basic data types (int, float, str, bool, None).

learning_objectives.title: Expressions and Operators
learning_objectives.description: Students can form and evaluate arithmetic, string, and boolean expressions using Python operators.

learning_objectives.title: Conditionals
learning_objectives.description: Students can write if/elif/else statements to control program flow based on conditions.

learning_objectives.title: Loops
learning_objectives.description: Students can write for and while loops, use range(), and apply loop control (break, continue).

learning_objectives.title: Functions
learning_objectives.description: Students can define and call functions with parameters, default values, and return values.

learning_objectives.title: Lists and Strings
learning_objectives.description: Students can create and manipulate lists and strings using indexing, slicing, and built-in methods.

learning_objectives.title: Dictionaries and Sets
learning_objectives.description: Students can create and use dictionaries and sets for key-value storage and membership testing.

learning_objectives.title: File I/O
learning_objectives.description: Students can read from and write to text files using open(), read(), write(), and context managers.

learning_objectives.title: Debugging and Error Handling
learning_objectives.description: Students can read tracebacks, identify common error types, and use try/except for exception handling.

learning_objectives.title: Program Design and Decomposition
learning_objectives.description: Students can design programs using top-down decomposition, identify reusable functions, and write docstrings.
